_comment: Scraper connection details per source group. Credentials here are public (Power BI Embedded resource keys). Per-hospital registry lives in hospitals.csv.

vahi_benchmarks._comment: System-wide and per-hospital VAHI 90th percentile (all ED categories). Quarter: Oct–Dec 2025. p90_all_mins = max across all hospitals (Box Hill 89m). Update each VAHI cycle alongside vahi_history_merged.csv.

monash_health._max_wait_audit: KNOWN DISCREPANCY (2026-04-28): Estimated Time for group_target=Adult gives a wait range (e.g. '2hr 46m - 6hr 50m'). The browser dashboard shows 8hr 51m for some campuses. This higher figure likely comes from (a) a separate 'Max Wait' visual querying all patients (not Adult-only), or (b) Paediatric patients included in an All-patients row. RESOLVED 2026-04-28: Implemented _parse_grouped_dsr_maxwait to scan all patient groups (Adult+Paeds) and return the highest wait upper-bound.
monash_health._timestamp_limitation: KNOWN LIMITATION (2026-04-30): LastUpdatedDisplay (G4) is a report-level property, NOT per-campus. Power BI returns the same timestamp for all three campuses (e.g., '18:31' for Casey, Clayton, AND Dandenong), but the webpage visual tiles show different per-campus values (e.g., Casey 18:26, Clayton 18:06, Dandenong 17:26). The visual tiles either: (a) compute timestamps client-side from a hidden measure, or (b) query a different entity. Current scraper extracts the report-level timestamp as the best available proxy. ValueDicts.D2 in the Power BI response contains only ONE timestamp value, confirming this is not a scraper bug. See /tmp/powerbi_debug_response.json for raw API evidence.
